name: Computer Science
description: Build the software, algorithms, and intelligent systems that power modern technology. Computer science students study programming, data structures, algorithms, operating systems, and computing theory — one of the highest-demand fields in the job market.

name: Data Engineering
description: Turn raw data into actionable insight using statistics, machine learning, and computational tools. Data science combines programming, math, and domain knowledge to find patterns and build models that drive real-world decisions — a fast-growing field for analytically minded students.

name: Computer Engineering
description: Bridge hardware and software to design complete computing systems — from embedded chips to data centers. Computer engineering covers circuits, computer architecture, operating systems, and hardware-software integration for students who want to build both the physical and digital layers of modern devices.

name: Mechanical Engineering
description: Design and build everything that moves — engines, robots, machines, and vehicles. One of A&M's largest and most versatile engineering majors, blending physics, design, and hands-on problem solving.

name: Electrical Engineering
description: Work with circuits, power systems, signals, and electronics that run today's devices and grids. Aggie EE students combine math, physics, and programming to design the electrical backbone of modern life.

name: Aerospace Engineering
description: Engineer aircraft, rockets, and spacecraft from the ground up. A&M's Aerospace program is a national leader, ideal for students captivated by flight, space, and high-performance vehicle design.

name: Chemical Engineering
description: Transform raw materials into fuels, medicines, and products through chemical processes. Aggie ChemE students apply chemistry, physics, and math to energy, biotech, and manufacturing challenges.

name: Civil Engineering
description: Plan and build the infrastructure society relies on — roads, bridges, water systems, and buildings. A cornerstone Aggie major for students who want visible, lasting impact on their communities.

name: Architectural Engineering
description: Bridge architecture and engineering to design safe, efficient, and sustainable buildings. Aggie ArchE students focus on the structural, electrical, and mechanical systems that bring buildings to life.
